title: Buildah build-using-dockerfile ONBUILD support
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

`buildah bud` does not appear to support Dockerfile ONBUILD commands.  As a drop-in replacement for Docker, Buildah is working great, except for this example.  I'd like to get developers to migrate away from the Docker daemon, and "build-using-dockerfile" is the painless way to get them started down that road.  The ONBUILD instruction is a somewhat widely adopted Dockerfile feature, however.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create a Dockerfile with ONBUILD instructions
2. Build an image from the Dockerfile with `buildah bud`
3. Create a Dockerfile "FROM" the image created in the last step
4. Build an image from the new Dockerfile with `buildah bud`
5. Verify that the ONBUILD instruction has not occurred in the new image

_Example:_
```
$ cat << EOF > Dockerfile
FROM fedora:28
RUN touch /foo
ONBUILD RUN touch /bar
EOF

$ sudo buildah bud -f Dockerfile -t onbuild-image .
STEP 1: FROM fedora:28
STEP 2: RUN touch /foo
STEP 3: ONBUILD RUN touch /bar
STEP 4: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay2.override_kernel_check=true]docker.io/library/onbuild-image:latest
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:72fcdba8cff9f105a61370d930d7f184702eeea634ac986da0105d8422a17028
Copying blob sha256:10d17c14659832afdc927fd9028f3cbf2c23159d112302f7b23f7f14bb7973f8
 165 B / 165 B [============================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:b1d7fb89c28825240f4af5bd32b976d0fbad8f3fae5f008588b12dea6bda5d2e
 1.45 KiB / 1.45 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

$ cat << EOF > Dockerfile-2
FROM onbuild-image
RUN touch /baz
EOF

$ sudo buildah bud -f Dockerfile-2 -t result-image .
STEP 1: FROM onbuild-image
STEP 2: RUN touch /baz
STEP 3: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay2.override_kernel_check=true]docker.io/library/result-image:latest
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:72fcdba8cff9f105a61370d930d7f184702eeea634ac986da0105d8422a17028
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:d492e2d4b31471965e7e1408946edefa8775bf8579070d0b57513ab5130583b8
Copying blob sha256:82320ff3b28c4bbcbd47a207942f8214c925db54163de7512378a070ecb44c75
 91 B / 91 B [==============================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:6575b7724204d8ac810dd2354798fb44286ef3573459b8aa7faf8e1c22285bd2
 1.75 KiB / 1.75 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

$ container=$(sudo buildah from result-image:latest)

$ sudo buildah run $container ls /bar
ls: cannot access '/bar': No such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Resulting second image does not have changes from ONBUILD tasks (/bar should exist inside the second image)

**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected resulting image to have changes from ONBUILD tasks (/bar should exist inside the second image)

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
$ rpm -q buildah
buildah-0.16-1.git82529cd.fc27.x86_64

```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
$ buildah version
Version:       0.16
Go Version:    go1.9.4
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    82529cd
Built:         Wed Apr  4 10:22:14 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
$ cat /etc/*release
Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven)
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="27 (Workstation Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=27
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 27 (Workstation Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:27"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=27
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=27
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Workstation Edition"
VARIANT_ID=workstation
Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven)
Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
$ uname -a
Linux krang.oit.duke.edu 4.16.5-200.fc27.x86_64 #1 SMP Fri Apr 27 19:05:44 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
$ cat /etc/containers/storage.conf 
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay2"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"

```

comments.body: Thanks for the issue @clcollins.  We'll take a look see and will add this if we can.
Closed with #722.  @clcollins if you find the functionality isn't up to snuff, please reopen or create a new issue as you feel appropriate.

@TomSweeneyRedHat - thanks!  I'll check it out.

I appreciate the fast turnaround on this!

Hey @TomSweeneyRedHat - 

I'm trying to test this out. It looks like the changes made it into Buildah 1.0, (unless I'm reading the merge incorrectly), but it's not working with the test case provided initially - with either the 1.0 RPM from the Fedora repos, or buildah installed from source from the master branch.

@clcollins what @rhatdan said.  In case it's of help, my test commands that worked for me below.  This is currently only available from master on GitHub.  Dan is working on a v1.1 kit that will include this, hopefully landing next week.


```
# # Removed most of the output from below
# buildah bud -f ~/Dockerfile --format=docker -t onbuild-image .

# buildah bud -f ~/Dockerfile-2 --format=docker -t result-image .

# ctr=$(buildah from result-image)

# buildah run $ctr ls /bar
/bar
```

The problem is OCI format, buildah's default does not support ONBUILD.
BTW You can also set an environment variable in 1.1 BUILDAH_FORMAT=docker, which would change the default format to be docker, for your experimenting.

Should buildah let the user know that a feature isn't supported by OCI when it hits this? And potentially stop execution because the user will get an inconsistent result?

eg. 'Dockerfile includes ONBUILD command which is unsupported in selected OCI image format. Use docker formatted image for full compatibility'

We can open that up as a proposal.  Personally I am not sure which ones are supported or not, So having them would be good. The problem would be blowing up Dockerfiles.  Can I base a OCI Image off of a Docker image that has OnBuilds?  Or should that fail?  I could see us failing when a user build an OCI image with a ONBUILD Command.
But when building by hand we can not tell what kind of image you are building until you commit.

Thanks @rhatdan & @TomSweeneyRedHat:  I recall seeing the `--format docker` argument as I was following the conversation for the merge, but forgot it was a thing when I tested.

Works beautifully when you actually do it correctly...

```
[chris@krang onbuild] $ sudo buildah bud -f Dockerfile-2 -t result-image --format docker .
STEP 1: FROM onbuild-image
STEP 2: RUN touch /bar
```
